source_title: Functional specialization of hippocampal somatostatin-expressing interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Directly addresses bistratified cell Tac1 marker and OLM cell Ndnf/Nkx2-1 markers; patch-seq/transgenic approach bridging transcriptomics to morphology for SST interneurons
summary: This study used combinatorial Cre/Flp mouse models to dissect functional subpopulations of CA1 hippocampal SST interneurons. The Sst;;Tac1 intersection revealed bistratified interneurons that preferentially synapse onto fast-spiking interneurons, while the Ndnf;;Nkx2-1 intersection identified OLM interneurons that predominantly target CA1 pyramidal neurons.
quotes: the Sst;;Tac1 intersection revealed a population of bistratified INs that preferentially synapsed onto fast-spiking interneurons (FS-INs) and were both necessary and sufficient to interrupt their firing
the Ndnf;;Nkx2-1 intersection identified a population of oriens lacunosum-moleculare (OLM) INs that predominantly targeted CA1 pyramidal neurons, avoiding FS-INs

source_title: Functional specialization of hippocampal somatostatin-expressing interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Directly addresses bistratified (Sst;;Tac1) vs OLM (Ndnf;;Nkx2-1, Chrna2) soma locations in stratum oriens, axonal projection patterns to radiatum vs LM; directly relevant to P3/P4 conflicts about Tac1 and Ndnf markers
summary: Sst;;Tac1-INs were located closer to the CA1 pyramidal layer with bilaminar (radiatum+oriens) axonal projections reminiscent of bistratified cells; Ndnf;;Nkx2-1-INs project axons to both oriens and LM; Chrna2-INs show almost exclusive projection to LM. The OLM category was constituted by Ndnf;;Nkx2-1-INs (10/13) and Chrna2-INs (16/19), while bistratified and oriens-oriens categories were almost exclusively represented by Sst;;Tac1 and Sst;;Nos1 respectively.
quotes: Sst;;Tac1-INs strongly targeted strata oriens and radiatum, a bilaminar morphology reminiscent of classically defined bistratified INs
the bistratified and oriens-oriens categories were disproportionately and almost exclusively represented by Sst;;Tac1-INs (n = 13/25) and Sst;;Nos1-INs (n = 12/15), respectively
While Sst;;Tac1-INs were located closer to the CA1 pyramidal layer, Ndnf;;Nkx2-1-INs and Chrna2-INs were found progressively deeper in O/A

source_title: Single-cell RNA-Seq characterization of anatomically identified OLM interneurons in different transgenic mouse lines
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises OLM cell soma location in str. oriens, axon to LM via scRNA-seq; relevant to P2 transcriptomics bridging; unexpected Npy expression in OLMs and uniformity of transcriptomic population
summary: Patch-seq study of OLM interneurons in Htr3a-Cre and Sst-Cre mouse lines. All morphologically confirmed OLM cells had fusiform somata residing in stratum oriens of CA1 with horizontally spanning dendritic branches and axons projecting to stratum lacunosum-moleculare. OLMs form a highly homogenous transcriptomic population; unexpectedly Npy was consistently expressed, and Htr3a was only expressed in ~10% of OLMs.
quotes: All cells had a fusiform cell soma residing in stratum oriens of hippocampal CA1 with horizontally spanning dendritic branches. Their axon frequently originated from a primary dendrite and projected towards the stratum lacunosum moleculare
we found a surprisingly consistent expression of Npy in OLMs, which was previously not associated with the identity of this type
OLMs constitute a highly homogenous transcriptomic population

source_title: Synaptic and dendritic architecture of different types of hippocampal somatostatin interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Directly compares OLM cells and hippocampo-septal (HS) cells: soma-dendritic architecture, synaptic inputs, activity patterns; addresses P5 (HS characterisation)
summary: Quantitative comparison of OLM and HS cells using scanning electron microscopy reconstructions, electrophysiology, and compartmental modelling. Both cell types have soma-dendritic architecture in stratum oriens but have different axonal targets. An OLM cell receives ~8400 synaptic inputs while an HS cell receives ~15600, with ~16% being GABAergic.
quotes: Two main types of SOM-containing cells in area CA1 of the hippocampus are oriens-lacunosum-moleculare (OLM) cells and hippocampo-septal (HS) cells

These cell types show many similarities in their soma-dendritic architecture, but they have different axonal targets, display different activity patterns in vivo and are thought to have distinct network functions
we estimated that an OLM cell receives about 8400, whereas an HS cell about 15600 synaptic inputs, about 16% of which are GABAergic

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
section: Identification and functional connectivity of IS3 cells
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises IS3 cells: VIP+/CR+ identity, soma location at O/A border, dendritic arbors, anatomical and functional properties; directly relevant to P3 IS subtypes
summary: IS3 cells in mouse hippocampus were characterized using VIP-eGFP and CR-Cre mouse models. IS3 cells innervate heavily the O/A border, co-express VIP and CR, and have unipolar dendritic arbors. Combined VIP+CR expression is necessary but insufficient alone; detailed anatomical analysis was crucial to distinguish IS3 from other VIP+/CR+ interneurons.
quotes: IS3 cells in the mouse hippocampus were similar to those described originally in the rat hippocampus. These cells innervated heavily the O/A border
although IS3 cells can be distinguished based on the coexpression of VIP and CR, VIP and CR alone are expressed by several distinct types of hippocampal interneurons
Their dendritic arbors were often unipolar (47% of cells), suggesting that these cells are preferentially recruited through the activation of the Schaffer collaterals

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Directly addresses Ivy cell vs NGC boundary question (P3); shows MGE vs CGE origin split for nNOS+ vs nNOS- NGCs; IvCs and nNOS+ NGCs share developmental origin and key markers
summary: Lineage analyses showed that ivy cells (IvCs) and nNOS+ neurogliaform cells (NGCs) derive from the MGE and have completely overlapping developmental, electrophysiological, morphological, and neurochemical properties, suggesting they constitute a single interneuron subtype despite differences in laminar organization. In contrast, nNOS- NGCs arise from CGE progenitors.
quotes: IvCs and nNOS+ NGCs have completely overlapping developmental, electrophysiological, morphological, and neurochemical properties, suggesting that these cells constitute a single unique interneuron subtype despite differences in laminar organization
classically defined NGCs can be subdivided into two groups based on nNOS expression and lineage with nNOS+ NGCs being derived from the MGE and nNOS- NGCs arising from CGE progenitors
both of these interneurons exhibit a LS phenotype and fail to express other classical interneuron markers such as PV, SOM, or CR

source_title: Inhibitory control of hippocampal inhibitory neurons
section: INTERNEURONS SPECIALIZED TO CONTROL OTHER INTERNEURONS
node_relevance_reason: Directly describes IS interneuron subtypes 1-3 in hippocampus; confirms interneuron-selective specialisation and describes subtypes
summary: Three distinct subtypes of IS interneurons were identified in the rat hippocampus and confirmed in human hippocampus. IS interneurons are specialized to control other GABAergic cells and may be unique to hippocampus and superficial cortical layers. The IS interneuron population receives input from long-range GABAergic projections and some interneuron types form autapses.
quotes: a combination of immunohistochemistry and anatomical analysis identified three distinct subtypes of IS interneurons in the rat hippocampus
the hippocampus and the superficial cortical layers (layers 1-3) may be the only cortical regions that possess such a highly specialized population of GABAergic cells
a subgroup of interneurons, the so-called interneuron-specific (IS) interneurons, specializes in innervating exclusively other GABAergic cells

source_title: Hippocampal GABAergic interneurons and memory
section: INTERNEURON TYPES AND MICROCIRCUITS ''Classic'' interneuron classifications
node_relevance_reason: Review covering all 9 cell types in node context including IS subtypes, ivy/NGC Lamp5 classification, CCK basket cells, and transcriptomic correspondence
summary: Comprehensive review of hippocampal interneuron classification covering PV (basket, AAC/chandelier, bistratified), SST (OLM, bistratified), Lamp5 (ivy, NGFC), Sncg (CCK), and VIP/IS interneuron subtypes. Notes that Lamp5 NGFCs paradoxically include both MGE-derived (nNOS+, Ivy/IvC) and CGE-derived subsets. Three IS interneuron subtypes exist, described as disinhibitory.
quotes: Lamp5 interneurons include ivy and neurogliaform cells (NGFCs). The ivy cell is the most common interneuron type in CA1; it has a distinct morphology with a relatively extensive axonal cloud extending over several hippocampal layers and co-expresses neuronal nitric oxide synthase (nNOS)

The majority of cells in the VIP subclass are interneuron-specific (IS), specifically targeting other interneurons rather than PCs. Three types of IS interneurons have been described, mostly in the hippocampus
Both display FS responses to depolarizing input and fire at high rates in vivo. In contrast, SOM cells typically have lower firing rates despite their low firing threshold

source_title: Discrete interneuron subsets participate in GluN1/GluN3A excitatory glycine receptor (eGlyR)-mediated regulation of hippocampal network activity throughout development and evolution
node_relevance_reason: Uses scRNA-seq to identify Ivy cell vs NGC subsets; confirms Lamp5+/Lhx6+ MGE-derived = Ivy cells; Lamp5+/Lhx6- CGE-derived = classical NGCs; nNOS in putative NGFCs in str. lacunosum moleculare
summary: scRNA-seq analysis confirmed that most Sst-INs express high levels of Grin3a. Among NGFCs, Grin3a is expressed in both Lamp5+/Lhx6+ MGE-derived (Ivy cells) and Lamp5+/Lhx6- CGE-derived subsets. In situ validated nNOS expression in putative NGFCs comprising the dominant interneuron subtype in stratum lacunosum moleculare.
quotes: Amongst NGFCs Grin3a is expressed in both Lamp5+/Lhx6+ MGE-derived subsets, also known as Ivy cells (IvyCs), and Lamp5+/Lhx6- caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE)-derived subsets

mClover-GluN3A labeled cells co-expressed Sst in stratum oriens and neuronal nitric oxide synthase (nNOS) in putative NGFCs that comprise the dominant interneuron subtype in stratum lacunosum moleculare

source_title: Transcriptional and morphological profiling of parvalbumin interneuron subpopulations in the mouse hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Patch-seq study of PV interneurons in CA1; directly addresses P4 conflict about transcriptomic continuity vs morphological/functional divergence in PV basket cells vs axo-axonic cells
summary: Single-cell RNA-seq combined with morphological analysis of parvalbumin interneurons (PV-INs) in CA1 shows high transcriptomic similarity among PV-INs with few genes showing divergent expression between morphologically different types. PV-INs show uniform synaptic cell adhesion molecule (CAM) profile, suggesting CAM expression does not reflect wiring specificity post-development.
quotes: we find high transcriptomic similarity among PV-INs, with few genes showing divergent expression between morphologically different types
PV-INs show a uniform synaptic cell adhesion molecule (CAM) profile, suggesting that CAM expression in mature PV cells does not reflect wiring specificity after development
while PV-INs differ in anatomy and in vivo activity, their continuous transcriptomic and homogenous biophysical landscapes are not predictive of these distinct identities

source_title: OLM interneurons differentially modulate CA3 and entorhinal inputs to hippocampal CA1 neurons
node_relevance_reason: Establishes Chrna2 as specific OLM cell marker; confirms OLM soma exclusively in SO of CA1; optogenetic evidence for OLM function in gating CA3 vs EC inputs
summary: Chrna2-Cre mouse line revealed that Chrna2+ cells are almost exclusively located in stratum oriens of CA1. Tomato+ cells were Sst+ and comprised 35.2% of CA1 Sst+ interneurons. OLM cells inhibit SLM dendritic compartments (reducing EC input) and disinhibit Schaffer collateral feedforward inhibition, facilitating CA3 input.
quotes: Tomato+ cells in the hippocampus were almost exclusively located in the SO of CA1 and subiculum
the vast majority (95.1%, 214/225 cells) of Tomato+ cells were also som+ and comprised a subpopulation (35.2%, 214/608) of CA1 som+ interneurons

OLM cells have a key role in gating the information flow in CA1, facilitating the transmission of intrahippocampal information (from CA3) while reducing the influence of extrahippocampal inputs (from the entorhinal cortex)

source_title: Functional specification of CCK+ interneurons by alternative isoforms of Kv4.3 auxiliary subunits
node_relevance_reason: Defines two firing phenotypes of CCK+ interneurons in CA3 via patch-seq approach; shows transcriptomic similarity can mask functional diversity due to alternative splicing
summary: Two distinct firing phenotypes of CCK+ interneurons exist in CA3 hippocampus: regular firing (RF) and transient outward rectifying (TOR), with distinct low-voltage-activated K currents. Despite very similar morphology and gene profiles, the two types differ in firing due to alternative isoforms of Kv4 auxiliary subunits (KChIP1 vs KChIP4e/DPP6S). Alternative splicing determines CCK+ interneuron functional identity.
quotes: Two firing phenotypes of CCK+INs in rat hippocampal CA3 area; either possessing a previously undetected membrane potential-dependent firing or regular firing phenotype, due to different low-voltage-activated potassium currents
the general intrinsic excitability, morphology and gene-profiles of the two types were surprisingly similar
the firing phenotypes were correlated with the presence of distinct isoforms of Kv4 auxiliary subunits (KChIP1 vs. KChIP4e and DPP6S)

source_title: Regulation of the Hippocampal Network by VGLUT3-Positive CCK-GABAergic Basket Cells
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises CCK basket cells with VGLUT3 co-expression; relevant to node context item 2 (CCK basket cell Vglut3+ marker); soma in str. pyramidale
summary: VGLUT3 is expressed by a subclass (~8.7%) of hippocampal GABAergic neurons that are CCK-positive basket cells. VGLUT3-positive terminals form axo-somatic symmetrical synapses in the pyramidal cell layer and co-express VIAAT (GABA transporter). Loss of VGLUT3 results in metaplastic shifts in synaptic plasticity and altered theta oscillations.
quotes: VGLUT3 is expressed by CCK-positive and more rarely by calbindin-positive interneurons
98 +/- 1% of VGLUT3-positive terminals co-expressed VIAAT but only 13 +/- 2% of VIAAT-positive terminals contained VGLUT3 in the CA1 pyramidal layer
VGLUT3-immunogold puncta were present over synaptic vesicle clusters in axon terminals forming axo-somatic symmetrical synapses

source_title: Distinct and synergistic feedforward inhibition of pyramidal cells by basket and bistratified interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Defines basket cells as fast-spiking perisomatic-targeting and bistratified cells as regular-spiking dendritic-targeting; addresses P1 soma locations and functional distinction
summary: Feedforward inhibition in CA1 is mediated by two distinct interneuron types: fast-spiking perisomatic-targeting basket cells and regular-spiking dendritic-targeting bistratified cells. These two FFI pathways modulate CA1PC threshold and gain respectively, and together synergistically extend the dynamic range of CA1 pyramidal cells.
quotes: FFI in CA1PCs is mediated by two physiologically and morphologically distinct GABAergic interneurons: fast-spiking, perisomatic-targeting basket cells and regular-spiking, dendritic-targeting bistratified cells
Separate FFI by basket and bistratified respectively modulated CA1PC threshold and gain
Concomitant FFI by both interneuron types synergistically extended the dynamic range of CA1PCs by buffering their spiking response to excitatory stimulation

source_title: Comparative density of CCK- and PV-GABA cells within the cortex and hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Compares CCK and PV basket cell distribution across hippocampal subregions; CCK cells outnumber PV cells in several hippocampal subregions
summary: Systematic intersectional genetic comparison of CCK-GABA and PV-GABA cell distribution across forebrain regions shows that while PV-GABA cells are more abundant overall, CCK-GABA cells outnumber PV-GABA cells in several hippocampal subregions. CCK cells predominate in secondary/association cortical areas while PV cells predominate in primary areas.
quotes: PV-GABA cells were more abundant overall, CCK-GABA cells outnumbered PV-GABA cells in several subregions of the hippocampus, medial prefrontal cortex and ventrolateral temporal cortex
CCK-GABA cells were relatively more abundant in secondary/association areas of the cortex (V2, S2, M2, and AudD/AudV) than they were in corresponding primary areas

source_title: Integration of electrophysiological recordings with single-cell RNA-seq data identifies novel neuronal subtypes
node_relevance_reason: Patch-seq method paper using CCK+ interneurons as model; bridges electrophysiology and transcriptomics; directly relevant to P2 transcriptomic bridging datasets
summary: Developed patch-seq approach combining patch-clamp electrophysiology with single-cell RNA-seq. Applied to CCK+ cortical interneurons as model system, demonstrating that electrophysiological continuum can be resolved into molecularly defined subtypes. Identified novel CCK+ interneuron subtypes based on combined data.
quotes: We focused on cholecystokinin (CCK)-containing(+) GABAergic interneurons because their morphological and molecular features are thought to form a quasi-continuum from axon- to dendrite-targeting interneurons
CCK+ interneurons are the plastic and dynamic gate-keepers of neuronal circuits

source_title: Functional networks of inhibitory neurons orchestrate synchrony in the hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: In vivo two-photon imaging of CA1 interneurons including PV basket/bistratified, NOS ivy cells, IS-1 and IS-3 interneurons; addresses P1 soma locations in pyramidal layer
summary: All-optical approach in CA1 simultaneously recorded interneurons and pyramidal cells in vivo. Interneurons included PV-expressing basket and bistratified cells in the pyramidal layer, NOS-expressing ivy cells, and 2 types of interneuron-selective interneurons (ISI 1 and 3) expressing calretinin. CA1 interneurons form a functionally interconnected network that promotes synchrony through disinhibition.
quotes: the most representative ones, the PV-expressing basket and bistratified cells, the NOS-expressing ivy cells and 2 types of interneuron-selective interneurons (ISI 1 and 3) that express calretinin
CA1 interneurons form a functionally interconnected network that promotes synchrony through disinhibition during awake immobility

source_title: Interneuronal mechanisms of hippocampal theta oscillations in a full-scale model of the rodent CA1 circuit
node_relevance_reason: Provides morphological data for ivy and neurogliaform cells collected by the authors; CCK basket cells noted to not receive monosynaptic excitation from local CA1 pyramidal cells
summary: Full-scale model of rodent CA1 circuit required new morphological data for ivy and neurogliaform cells (filled in hippocampal slices). CCK+ basket cells are noted to never receive direct monosynaptic excitation from local CA1 pyramidal cells. Connectivity estimates for all interneuron types including bistratified, OLM, neurogliaform, ivy, and basket cells were calculated.
quotes: As there were no data available for ivy and neurogliaform cells, we performed the necessary experiments in our lab by filling ivy and neurogliaform cells in hippocampal CA1 slices from Wistar rats and then measuring their somatic area and dendrites
CCK+ basket cells have never been observed to receive direct monosynaptic excitement from local CA1 pyramidal cells
O-LM cells receive almost all of their excitatory connections from local

source_title: High and low expression of the hyperpolarization activated current (I h) in mouse CA1 stratum oriens interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Discusses classification of hippocampal interneurons in stratum oriens; OLM cells and LTH cell soma locations; raises question about OLM subtype boundaries
summary: Identifies two classes of stratum oriens interneurons in CA1 based on spike frequency adaptation and Ih density: LTH cells (strong SFA, high Ih) and OLM cells. Both are labeled in SST:cre Ai14 mice. The LTH cell appears to be a previously uncharacterized type of CA1 stratum oriens interneuron distinct from OLM cells based on physiological clustering.
quotes: Within the hippocampus, the CA1 region alone contains at least 28 previously described types of inhibitory interneurons located throughout all layers: stratum oriens, stratum pyramidale, stratum radiatum, and stratum lacunosum-moleculare
Strong and weak SFA cells were labeled in hippocampal slices from SST:cre Ai14 mice suggesting both cells express somatostatin
While the cell body and dendrites of LTH neurons reside within stratum oriens, their role in the hippocampal circuit remains unknown

source_title: Novel Hippocampal Interneuronal Subtypes Identified Using Transgenic Mice That Express Green Fluorescent Protein in GABAergic Interneurons
section: Hippocampal EGFP-expressing cells are somatostatinexpressing GABAergic interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Characterises OLM cells: soma in SO of CA1 with oval-shaped, horizontally oriented soma, dendrites parallel to strata borders; Sst expression confirms identity
summary: In GIN transgenic mice, EGFP-expressing hippocampal interneurons are Sst+ GABAergic cells. In CA1, most are OLM cells with oval-shaped somata in stratum oriens and dendrites running parallel to strata borders. Two new interneuronal subtypes (R-LM and P-LM) were identified as Sst+. All three subtypes show fast-spiking and depolarizing sag.
quotes: a noticeable feature of hippocampal EGFP-expressing interneurons in area CA1 of GIN mice was the localization of their often oval-shaped somata to SO, with dendrites running parallel to the strata borders. Such morphology is characteristic of O-LM cells
In area CA1, the vast majority of these interneurons were comprised of O-LM cells and two newly identified interneuronal subtypes, R-LM and P-LM cells

source_title: Electrophysiological and Morphological Characterization of Chrna2 Cells in the Subiculum and CA1 of the Hippocampus: An Optogenetic Investigation
node_relevance_reason: Confirms Chrna2 as specific marker for CA1 OLM interneurons; soma and dendritic trees in str. oriens; axon in str. lacunosum-moleculare
summary: Confirms that CA1 OLM interneurons are GABAergic, somatostatin-expressing cells with soma and dendritic trees in stratum oriens and axons arborizing in stratum lacunosum-moleculare. Chrna2 was identified as a specific genetic marker for CA1 OLM interneurons by Leao et al. 2012.
quotes: these CA1 GABAergic, somatostatin (Som)-expressing interneurons are named for their distinctive morphology: their soma and dendritic trees are located in the stratum oriens and their axons extend directly out to arborize in the stratum lacunosum-moleculare
the nicotinic acetylcholine receptor Chrna2 was identified as a specific genetic marker for CA1 OLM interneurons

source_title: Local and long-range GABAergic circuits in hippocampal area CA1 and their link to Alzheimer's disease
node_relevance_reason: Comprehensive CA1 interneuron classification including molecular markers for all major subtypes; CA1 contains >21 types
summary: Review of CA1 GABAergic interneuron diversity: CA1 contains more than 21 types classified by transcriptome, morphology, molecular markers, intrinsic physiology, and postsynaptic targets. Traditional molecular categories include PV, VIP, CCK, SST, NPY, and NDNF/nNOS interneurons. Transgenic animal lines based on molecular markers provide selective access to major interneuron populations.
quotes: area CA1 contains more than 21 types of GABAergic INs

GABAergic INs have been traditionally categorized as parvalbumin (PV), vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), cholecystokinin (CCK), somatostatin (SST), neuropeptide Y (NPY), and neuron-derived neurotrophic factor (NDNF) and/or nitric oxide synthase (nNOS), among others

source_title: Molecular Specialization of GABAergic Synapses on the Soma and Axon in Cortical and Hippocampal Circuit Function and Dysfunction
node_relevance_reason: Describes CCK+ basket cells targeting soma and proximal dendrites; CB1 receptor expression; PV basket cells vs CCK basket cells distinction
summary: CCK-positive basket cells target the soma and proximal dendrites of cortical and hippocampal pyramidal cells and do not appear to innervate the axon initial segment. They express CB1 (cannabinoid receptor type 1) which modulates GABA release presynaptically. Most hippocampal interneurons express either CCK or PV.
quotes: CCK positive basket cells target the soma and proximal dendrites of cortical and hippocampal pyramidal cells, and do not appear to innervate the AIS
Proportions of CCK basket cells express the ionotropic serotonin receptor (5-HT3) and the metabotropic Cannabinoid receptor type 1 (CB1), which modulate GABA release from the presynaptic terminal
the majority of interneurons in these regions express either the neuropeptide cholecystokinin or the calcium binding protein parvalbumin

source_title: Dendritic inhibition mediated by O-LM and bistratified interneurons in the hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Addresses OLM and bistratified cells in the context of dendritic inhibition; basket cells in str. pyramidale with perisomatic synapses
summary: Reviews hippocampal interneuron diversity focusing on CA1 PV+ basket cells (in str. pyramidale, perisomatic) and dendritic-targeting cells (OLM, bistratified). A main parameter for CA1 interneuron classification is the specific subcellular domain they target on pyramidal cells. Basket cells extend axons to stratum pyramidale forming characteristic perisomatic basket structures.
quotes: A main parameter used for classification of CA1 interneurons that target pyramidal neurons is the specific subcellular domain on which they form GABAergic synapses

basket cells extend their axonal arbor to the stratum pyramidale. In fact, pyramidal neuron somata are contacted extensively by GABAergic synapses from these cells forming characteristic basket-like structures

source_title: Behavior-dependent activity patterns of GABAergic long-range projecting neurons in the rat hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises hippocampo-septal cells: SOM-expressing long-range projecting neurons in CA1 with projections to medial septum; distinguishes from local SST cells; key for P5
summary: In CA1, at least 5 distinct types of SOM-expressing GABAergic neurons exist. In addition to locally-terminating SOM interneurons (bistratified, OLM), a diverse population of long-range SOM-expressing GABAergic neurons project to the medial septum and/or retrohippocampal cortical areas. These hippocampo-septal and long-range projecting neurons were studied for their activity patterns.
quotes: In the rat hippocampal CA1 area, at least five distinct types of SOM-expressing GABAergic neuron have been defined

In addition to these exclusively locally terminating SOM-expressing interneurons, a diverse population of long-range SOM-expressing GABAergic neurons send projections to the medial septum and/or retrohippocampal cortical areas

source_title: Septo-hippocampal interaction
node_relevance_reason: Directly addresses hippocampo-septal cells: GABAergic neurons in str. oriens that project to medial septum, forming reciprocal long-range GABAergic circuit
summary: GABAergic neurons in hippocampus that project to the medial septum are located in stratum oriens and form a reciprocal long-range GABAergic septo-hippocampal circuit. Many long-range GABAergic neurons form both local synapses in CA1 and en passant synapses in remote areas; their axons are highly myelinated. These neurons receive septal GABAergic input and can receive input from the same cell they project to.
quotes: GABAergic neurons in the hippocampus, which project to the medial septum, are located in the stratum oriens of the hippocampus
they form a reciprocal long-range GABAergic septo-hippocampal circuit. Many long-range GABAergic neurons simultaneously form local synapses in CA1 and en passant synapses in several remote areas
The long-range projecting axons of the GABAergic neurons are highly myelinated, which argues for a specific role in the immediate synchronization and functional binding of remote areas

source_title: Differential Expression of Group I Metabotropic Glutamate Receptors in Functionally Distinct Hippocampal Interneurons
section: Oriens-alveus interneurons respond differentially to mGluR activation
node_relevance_reason: Characterises OLM cells via mGluR1 expression; soma at str. oriens/alveus border; axon in SLM; Sst+ confirmed
summary: Type I interneurons (OLM cells) have large horizontally oriented soma at the border of stratum oriens and alveus. Reconstruction confirmed axonal arborizations in stratum lacunosum-moleculare and horizontal dendritic tree in stratum oriens. Four type of interneurons were characterised by response to mGluR activation.
quotes: Type I interneurons had large horizontally oriented cell somata located at the border of stratum oriens and the alveus, indicating that these cells were most likely identical with the previously described somatostatin-positive oriens-lacunosum moleculare (O-LM) cells
Reconstruction of type I interneurons revealed their horizontally oriented dendritic tree in stratum oriens and their axonal arborizations in stratum lacunosum-moleculare

source_title: Molecular and Electrophysiological Characterization of GFP-Expressing CA1 Interneurons in GAD65-GFP Mice
node_relevance_reason: Characterises CGE-derived CA1 interneurons in GAD65-GFP mice; NPY+ ivy/neurogliaform cells are the largest subgroup; addresses NGF/ivy cell molecular profile with NPY and reelin
summary: GAD65-GFP cells in CA1 are predominantly CGE-derived interneurons. The largest subgroup expresses NPY (42% of GFP cells), and these cells almost never co-express somatostatin. NPY-expressing GFP cells include ivy cells (multipolar in all layers) and neurogliaform cells (tangentially oriented in upper layers). Reelin expression was slightly higher in NPY+ GFP cells.
quotes: The largest subgroup consisted of GFP cells that expressed NPY. 42+/-3% of GFP cells expressed NPY
the subset of GFP-expressing cells did not co-express somatostatin (304/310; 98%)
The labeled cell types correspond well to previously described NPY-positive multipolar cells, often referred to as Ivy cells and neurogliaform cells

source_title: Subcellular sequencing of single neurons reveals the dendritic transcriptome of GABAergic interneurons
section: Identification of cell types based on the somatic transcriptome
node_relevance_reason: Transcriptomic cell type identification in cultured hippocampal neurons; mentions basket-bistratified, HS, OLM soma locations; addresses transcriptomic bridging but in vitro context
summary: Subcellular sequencing identifies GABAergic neuron types in hippocampal cultures via transcriptomics. GABAergic 1 neurons integrate with basket-bistratified, HS, and OLM interneurons in tissue datasets, with somata in str. pyramidale and complex dendritic arborizations. GABAergic 2/3 neurons match RLMb and neurogliaform interneurons with somata at sr/slm border.
quotes: The hippocampal cells they most resemble, Basket-bistratified, HS and OLM interneurons, have their somata in the stratum pyramidale (sp) of the hippocampus
their transcriptomes were closest to RLMb and Neuroglialform interneurons whose somata are located at the border between the stratum radiatum (sr) and the slm and exhibit short dendrites

source_title: Role of Ionotropic Glutamate Receptors in Long-Term Potentiation in Rat Hippocampal CA1 Oriens-Lacunosum Moleculare Interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Confirms diversity of stratum oriens interneurons beyond OLM; mentions bistratified, OLM, hippocampo-septal, and other horizontal cells in str. oriens
summary: Although stratum oriens horizontal interneurons are often studied as a single group, they include multiple distinct cell types (O-LM, bistratified, hippocampo-septal, etc.), each with unique properties and characteristic circuit placement. Different interneuron types show heterogeneous synaptic plasticity, likely reflecting cell-type-specific patterns.
quotes: horizontal interneurons in stratum oriens of the hippocampal CA1 area are often studied as a single group of interneurons, they include several cell types in addition to O-LM cells
interneurons in this layer are diverse and fall into several different categories. Each shows unique properties and a characteristic place in the circuitry

source_title: Cell type-specific expression of Eps8 in the mouse hippocampus
section: Specific subpopulations of hippocampal interneurons expressing Eps8
node_relevance_reason: Characterises Eps8+ interneurons as calbindin+/CCK+ CA1 interneurons in str. radiatum; Eps8 highest in str. radiatum
summary: Eps8-expressing cells in CA1 are GABAergic interneurons predominantly in stratum radiatum, with Eps8 positivity highest in radiatum (44%) and progressively lower in LM, oriens, and pyramidale. All Eps8+ cells co-express calbindin but not parvalbumin or calretinin. The majority co-express CCK, suggesting they belong to CCK/calbindin-expressing interneurons.
quotes: The proportion of GFP-expressing interneurons coexpressing Eps8 was highest in the stratum radiatum (44%) and less in the stratum lacunosum-moleculare, stratum oriens and stratum pyramidale (28%, 16% and 13%)
all Eps8-expressing cells were positive for calbindin
no Eps8-expressing cells were found that expressed parvalbumin or calretinin

source_title: Comprehensive Estimates of Potential Synaptic Connections in Local Circuits of the Rodent Hippocampal Formation by Axonal-Dendritic Overlap
section: Number of contacts per connected neuron pair
node_relevance_reason: Provides connectivity estimates for interneuron types including bistratified, ivy, neurogliaform; compares to earlier CA1 circuit model
summary: Comprehensive connectivity analysis of hippocampal formation circuits. The paper compares data with Bezaire and Soltesz (2013) for 7 CA1 neuron types including bistratified, ivy, neurogliaform, pyramidal, and perforant path-associated cells. Correlation between studies (R=0.73) validates the methodology.
quotes: This earlier study focused on seven dendritic-targeting neuron types: Pyramidal, Bistratified, Ivy, Neurogliaform, Perforant Path-Associated, Schaffer Collateral-Associated

source_title: Molecular and electrophysiological features of GABAergic neurons in the dentate gyrus reveal limited homology with cortical interneurons
section: Hilar interneurons show limited homology with cortical GABAergic interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Dentate gyrus interneurons, not CA1; general interneuron classification framework applicable but does not directly address the 9 cell types
summary: Most GABAergic interneurons in neocortex, hippocampus and striatum share common developmental origin. Three major families: (1) soma/axo-axonic PV-expressing fast-discharging cells, (2) dendrite-targeting SOM-expressing cells, and (3) neurogliaform NPY/Reelin-expressing cells. Cortex/hippocampus also contains CCK soma-targeting and VIP IS interneurons.
quotes: they tend to include the same 3 major families: (1) soma and axo-axonic neurons showing fast discharge and expressing PV, (2) dendrite targeting interneurons expressing SOM and (3) neurogliaform neurons expressing NPY/Reelin

source_title: Morpho-physiological Criteria Divide Dentate Gyrus Interneurons into Classes
node_relevance_reason: Dentate gyrus interneurons, not CA1; general principle of discrete interneuron classes vs continuum
summary: Cluster analysis of dentate gyrus GABAergic cells identified five interneuron classes with distinct morpho-physiological parameters, supporting the interneuron class hypothesis. Strong correlations were found between morphological and physiological properties, suggesting a direct structure-function link.
quotes: Cluster analysis on the basis of morphometric variables revealed that dentate gyrus interneurons fall into five classes with each class characterized by a distinct set of morpho-physiological parameters

source_title: Immunofluorescent visualization of mouse interneuron subtypes
node_relevance_reason: Describes antibody validation for parvalbumin, calbindin, somatostatin, calretinin in mouse hippocampus; 21 interneuron subtypes based on expression/morphology/physiology
summary: Systematic evaluation of commercial antibodies for immunofluorescent visualization of parvalbumin, calbindin, somatostatin, and calretinin in mouse hippocampus and spinal cord. The hippocampus has 21 different subtypes of GABAergic interneurons based on marker expression, morphology, and electrophysiology.
quotes: In the mammalian hippocampus, these are divided into 21 different subtypes of GABAergic interneurons based on their expression of different markers, morphology and their electrophysiological properties

source_title: Interneuron diversity in the rat dentate gyrus: An unbiased in vitro classification
node_relevance_reason: Dentate gyrus interneurons; general methodology but not directly relevant to the 9 CA1/hippocampal cell types
summary: Unbiased classification of GABAergic interneurons in the rat dentate gyrus using whole-cell patch-clamp, intracellular labelling, and unsupervised cluster analysis reveals at least 13 distinct morpho-physiological types.
quotes: This analysis reveals that DG INs divide into at least 13 distinct morpho-physiological types which reflect the complexity of the local IN network

source_title: Cell Type-Specific Expression of Acid-Sensing Ion Channels in Hippocampal Interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Distinguishes fast-spiking basket cells vs OLM cells as two main functional classes; soma locations and functions described
summary: Two functionally distinct classes of hippocampal GABAergic interneurons: soma-targeting fast-spiking basket cells (in str. pyramidale) that control spike initiation and dendrite-targeting OLM cells (innervating distal dendritic domains) that regulate dendritic spikes and synaptic plasticity. These two subtypes also differ in neurochemical contents, Ca2+ buffering, ion channels, and transmitter receptors.
quotes: fast-spiking basket cells (BCs), control the spike initiation of principal neurons via axonal innervations onto perisomatic areas

dendrite-targeting inhibitory interneurons, such as oriens lacunosum-moleculare (O-LM) cells, regulate dendritic Na+ or Ca2+ spikes and synaptic plasticity by innervating dendritic domains of principal neurons

source_title: Hippocampal GABAergic interneurons and memory
section: INTERNEURON TYPES AND MICROCIRCUITS ''Classic'' interneuron classifications
node_relevance_reason: Describes IS subtypes (3 types), CCK cell diversity, and SST PV co-expression in bistratified and OLM; directly addresses P3/P4
summary: Reviews diverse CCK interneuron types extending beyond Sncg transcriptomic cluster. Three IS interneuron subtypes described as mostly hippocampal with likely disinhibitory roles. Bistratified and OLM cells reported to co-express SOM and PV. Both AAC and FS basket cells display fast-spiking responses.
quotes: These two interneuron types have been reported to co-express SOM and PV

The majority of cells in the VIP subclass are interneuron-specific (IS), specifically targeting other interneurons rather than PCs. Three types of IS interneurons have been described, mostly in the hippocampus

source_title: Hippocampal GABAergic interneurons and memory
node_relevance_reason: Concludes review on interneuron diversity and gaps in knowledge; up to 100 transcriptomic types described; function of most types still uninvestigated
summary: Despite much progress, a complete understanding of both single-neuron characteristics and functional roles of hippocampal interneurons remains incomplete. Single-cell transcriptomic datasets have led to extensive taxonomy with up to 100 types described, but how these relate to traditional classifications remains unclear.
quotes: recent single-cell transcriptomic datasets have led to a more extensive taxonomy of hippocampal interneurons, with up to 100 types having been described, the functional significance of these transcriptomic types remains mostly uninvestigated
there is still much work to be done to determine how these transcriptomic cell types relate to the more traditional classifications such as morphological or molecular markers

source_title: Emergence of non-canonical parvalbumin-containing interneurons in hippocampus of a murine model of type I lissencephaly
node_relevance_reason: Wild-type CA1 PV interneurons show two physiological subtypes (80% FS, 20% NFS) and four morphological subtypes; relevant to P1 soma locations and P4 PV+ interneuron subtypes
summary: Wild-type CA1 PV+ interneurons comprise 80% fast-spiking and 20% non-fast-spiking physiological subtypes and four morphological subtypes. In Pafah1b1 mutant mice, an intermediate-spiking subtype emerges. Single-cell nuclear RNAseq of PV+ INTs revealed genes related to morphogenesis, excitability, and synapse formation.
quotes: WT PV+INTs consist of two physiological subtypes (80% fast-spiking (FS), 20% non-fast-spiking (NFS)) and four morphological subtypes

source_title: Synaptic and dendritic architecture of different types of hippocampal somatostatin interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Directly characterises OLM and hippocampo-septal (HS) cells in CA1 with detailed anatomical, electrophysiological and synaptic input comparison; highly relevant to P1 (soma location), P5 (HS cell) and bistratified cell nodes
summary: Two major SOM-containing interneuron types in CA1 are OLM and hippocampo-septal (HS) cells; both share soma-dendritic architecture but differ in axonal targets and in vivo activity. Quantitative 3D electron microscopy reconstructions show OLM cells receive ~8400 synaptic inputs while HS cells receive ~15600, with ~16% GABAergic in both. Data include patch-clamp electrophysiology and compartmental modelling enabling precise comparison of intrinsic properties.
quotes: Two main types of SOM-containing cells in area CA1 of the hippocampus are oriens-lacunosum-moleculare (OLM) cells and hippocampo-septal (HS) cells. These cell types show many similarities in their soma-dendritic architecture, but they have different axonal targets, display different activity patterns in vivo and are thought to have distinct network functions.
we estimated that an OLM cell receives about 8400, whereas an HS cell about 15600 synaptic inputs, about 16% of which are GABAergic

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
section: Identification and functional connectivity of IS3 cells
node_relevance_reason: Direct characterisation of IS3 cells including soma location, axonal targets, marker co-expression (VIP+CR), morphological subtypes, and functional connectivity to OLM cells; directly addresses P3 (IS subtype split) and IS interneuron node
summary: IS3 cells in mouse hippocampus co-express VIP and CR and have somas in stratum pyramidale or radiatum, sending axons to the oriens/alveus to contact OLM cells preferentially. The study confirms that IS3 cells preferentially innervate the O/A border (with occasional RAD and PYR branches) and have unipolar (47%) dendritic arbors consistent with preferential recruitment through Schaffer collateral input. Multiple criteria are required to discriminate IS cells from other GABAergic interneurons.
quotes: IS3 cells in the mouse hippocampus were similar to those described originally in the rat hippocampus. These cells innervated heavily the O/A border, although occasional axonal branches were found in the RAD and PYR. Their dendritic arbors were often unipolar (47% of cells), suggesting that these cells are preferentially recruited through the activation of the Schaffer collaterals
although IS3 cells can be distinguished based on the coexpression of VIP and CR, VIP and CR alone are expressed by several distinct types of hippocampal interneurons

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
node_relevance_reason: Abstract confirms IS3 cells express VIP+CR, soma in PYR/RAD, axon to O/A, target OLM cells preferentially; VIP basket cells do NOT connect with O/A interneurons; relevant to IS interneuron node and P3
summary: IS3 cells (VIP+/CR+) in CA1 O/A preferentially innervate OLM cells through dendritic synapses; VIP-positive basket cells provide perisomatic inhibition to pyramidal neurons with asynchronous GABA release and are NOT connected to O/A interneurons. IS3-OLM synapses have small amplitude and low release probability but summated efficiently at high frequency, and synchronous IS3 activity controls OLM firing rate and timing.
quotes: type 3 IS (IS3) cells that coexpress the vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) and calretinin contact several distinct types of interneurons within the hippocampal CA1 stratum oriens/alveus (O/A), with preferential innervation of oriens-lacunosum moleculare cells (OLMs) through dendritic synapses
VIP-positive basket cells provided perisomatic inhibition to CA1 pyramidal neurons with the asynchronous GABA release and were not connected with O/A interneurons

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
node_relevance_reason: Distinguishes IS1 from IS3 subtypes by anatomy: IS1 has cell body in O/A, PYR, or RAD with wide-projecting axon in RAD/PYR and dendro-dendritically connected clusters; IS3 has soma in RAD/PYR with axon projecting to O/A; directly supports P3 IS subtype split
summary: Using CR-Cre mice, at least two subtypes of CR-positive interneurons were identified in CA1 RAD/PYR. The first (IS1) has soma in O/A, PYR, or RAD, multipolar dendrites, axon mainly in RAD/PYR, dendro-dendritically connected clusters, and high input resistance (~483 MΩ). The second (IS3) has soma in RAD/PYR, unipolar/bipolar dendrites, and axon projecting to O/A. The two subtypes could only be distinguished anatomically.
quotes: One group of CR-positive cells had a cell body located within the O/A, PYR, or RAD; a multipolar dendritic tree; and a widely projecting axon that mostly occupied the CA1 RAD and PYR (ϳ65%; Fig. 6C1). These cells had a high input resistance (482.5 Ϯ 98.2 M⍀; n ϭ 4)

In several cases (n ϭ 5 slices/2 animals), the dendrites of 2-3 CR-positive cells of this subtype were found to form dendro-dendritically connected clusters (Fig. 6C1, top inset), which is a hallmark of the type I interneuron-specific (IS1) interneuron described previously in the rat CA1 hippocampus

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
section: Identification and functional connectivity of IS3 cells
node_relevance_reason: Describes IS1 cells as CR+, heterogeneous in firing properties, with soma in RAD/PYR and wide RAD/PYR axon; notes IS1 group may contain more than one subtype; supports P3
summary: Using the CR-Cre mouse model, IS1-type CR+ interneurons were characterised in RAD/PYR: they showed remarkable anatomical diversity, with cell bodies in different layers and axons mostly in RAD/PYR, high input resistance, and heterogeneous firing properties. The study concludes that more than one subtype of putative IS1 cells likely exists within this CR+ group.
quotes: This subtype of IS cells exhibited a remarkable anatomical diversity, with cell bodies that occupied different layers and an axon that projected mostly within the RAD and PYR. Similar to IS3 cells, IS1 cells exhibited a high input resistance but were more heterogeneous in terms of firing properties. Therefore, more than one subtype of putative IS1 cells is likely to be found within this group of CR-positive interneurons.

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
node_relevance_reason: Introduces IS cell classification: IS1 (CR+), IS3 (VIP+/CR+, soma in PYR/RAD, axon to O/A), targets OLM cells; VIP expression by IS cells noted; relevant to IS interneuron node P3
summary: IS cells were subdivided into three subtypes with distinct anatomical and neurochemical features. IS3 somas are in stratum pyramidale or radiatum, axons go to O/A to contact interneurons, preferentially mGluR1a+ OLM cells. IS3 cells target OLMs, bistratified cells, and oriens-oriens interneurons, and synchronous IS3 activity controls postsynaptic cell firing rate and timing.
quotes: The somas of these cells are located in the stratum pyramidale (PYR) or radiatum (RAD) and send their axons to the oriens-alveus (O/A) to contact other interneurons located in this area, preferentially metabotropic glutamate receptor 1a (mGluR1a)-positive oriens-lacunosum moleculare interneurons (OLMs)

source_title: Functional specialization of hippocampal somatostatin-expressing interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Directly bridges transcriptomic Sst-IN subtypes to morpho-functional identities using combinatorial Cre/Flp intersectional approach; identifies Sst;;Tac1 intersection as bistratified cells and Ndnf;;Nkx2-1 as OLM cells; addresses P2 (bridging datasets), P4 (Tac1 for bistratified, PV+ OLM subpopulation, Ndnf protein)
summary: Using four mouse intersectional models (Sst;;Tac1, Ndnf;;Nkx2-1, and others) in CA1, functionally distinct Sst-IN subpopulations were identified that tile the morpho-functional parameter space of the Sst superfamily. The Sst;;Tac1 intersection revealed bistratified INs that preferentially synapse onto fast-spiking interneurons (FS-INs) and can interrupt their firing. The Ndnf;;Nkx2-1 intersection identified OLM INs that predominantly target CA1 pyramidal neurons and avoid FS-INs.
quotes: the Sst;;Tac1 intersection revealed a population of bistratified INs that preferentially synapsed onto fast-spiking interneurons (FS-INs) and were both necessary and sufficient to interrupt their firing
the Ndnf;;Nkx2-1 intersection identified a population of oriens lacunosum-moleculare (OLM) INs that predominantly targeted CA1 pyramidal neurons, avoiding FS-INs

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Provides key evidence that IvC and nNOS+ NGC share overlapping developmental, electrophysiological, morphological, and neurochemical properties; nNOS+ NGCs are MGE-derived while nNOS- NGCs are CGE-derived; directly addresses P3 (IvC vs NGC boundary) and P4 (Nos1 universality in NGC)
summary: Ivy cells (IvCs) and nNOS+ neurogliaform cells (NGCs) have completely overlapping developmental, electrophysiological, morphological, and neurochemical properties, suggesting they constitute a single unique interneuron subtype despite differences in laminar organisation. nNOS+ NGCs are MGE-derived (under Nkx2-1 control), while nNOS− NGCs are CGE-derived. The laminar positions of IvCs and NGCs may reflect different functional roles despite shared properties.
quotes: our findings reveal that IvCs and nNOS ϩ NGCs have completely overlapping developmental, electrophysiological, morphological, and neurochemical properties, suggesting that these cells constitute a single unique interneuron subtype despite differences in laminar organization
classically defined NGCs can be subdivided into two groups based on nNOS expression and lineage with nNOS ϩ NGCs being derived from the MGE and nNOS Ϫ NGCs arising from CGE progenitors

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Abstract confirms NGCs and IvCs share neurochemical and electrophysiological signatures (NPY+, late spiking), both derive from MGE under Nkx2-1, but a nNOS- NGC subset arises from CGE; directly addresses P3 and P4
summary: NGCs and IvCs represent the main nNOS+ hippocampal interneurons; they share NPY expression and late-spiking firing patterns. Lineage analyses show that nNOS+ IvCs and NGCs both derive from MGE progenitors under Nkx2-1 control, while a distinct nNOS- NGC subpopulation arises from CGE progenitors.
quotes: nNOS+ IvCs and NGCs are both derived from medial ganglionic eminence (MGE) progenitors under control of the transcription factor Nkx2-1

a subset of NGCs lacking nNOS arises from caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE) progenitors. Thus, while nNOS+ NGCs and IvCs arise from MGE progenitors, a CGE origin distinguishes a discrete population of nNOS− NGCs

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Addresses nNOS expression across NGC subtypes: nNOS+ NGCs are the majority in hippocampus (MGE-derived), nNOS- NGCs are a minor CGE population; contrasts with neocortex where CGE-derived NGCs dominate; directly addresses P4 Nos1 universality
summary: In hippocampus, the nNOS+ (MGE-derived) subset represents the majority of all NGCs, with only minor contribution from CGE-derived (nNOS-) NGCs — the reverse of the neocortex. NPY expression is activity-dependent, which may explain similar NPY levels across both NGC groups despite different origins. The study cannot rule out that IvCs and nNOS+ NGCs perform distinct functions due to their different laminar positions.
quotes: we found that the nNOS ϩ subset, hence MGE derived, represents the majority of all hippocampal NGCs with a minor contribution from CGE-derived NGCs. This contrasts with findings in the neocortex in which fate-mapping evidence supports a dominant CGE origin for NGCs

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Historically NGCs were considered NPY+/nNOS+ homogeneous; this study's NPY-GFP characterisation reveals nNOS+ majority vs nNOS- minor CGE subset; supports P3 boundary between IvC and NGC based on nNOS
summary: Historically NGCs were viewed as functionally homogeneous with NPY and frequent nNOS co-expression. In the hippocampus, the nNOS+ (MGE-derived) NGC subset is the majority. CGE-derived nNOS- NGCs are a minor population possibly explaining lower nNOS levels seen in neocortex. Regional difference in NGC origins between hippocampus and neocortex is noted.
quotes: Historically, NGCs have been viewed as a functionally homogenous interneuron subtype characterized by the expression of NPY with frequent coexpression of nNOS

source_title: Transcriptional and morphological profiling of parvalbumin interneuron subpopulations in the mouse hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Patch-seq study of PV+ interneurons in mouse CA1 showing transcriptomic continuity across morphologically distinct subtypes; directly addresses P2 (bridging datasets) and P4 (PV+ subpopulation transcriptomic boundaries including axo-axonic vs basket)
summary: Patch-seq applied to morphologically identified PV+ interneurons in mouse CA1 showed high transcriptomic similarity across subtypes (basket, axo-axonic, bistratified), with few genes showing divergent expression between morphological types. PV-INs show a uniform synaptic cell adhesion molecule (CAM) profile, suggesting CAM expression in mature PV cells does not reflect wiring specificity after development. Results indicate transcriptomic and biophysical landscapes are continuous and not predictive of distinct morphological identities.
quotes: we find high transcriptomic similarity among PV-INs, with few genes showing divergent expression between morphologically different types
PV-INs show a uniform synaptic cell adhesion molecule (CAM) profile, suggesting that CAM expression in mature PV cells does not reflect wiring specificity after development
while PV-INs differ in anatomy and in vivo activity, their continuous transcriptomic and homogenous biophysical landscapes are not predictive of these distinct identities

source_title: Discrete interneuron subsets participate in GluN1/GluN3A excitatory glycine receptor (eGlyR)-mediated regulation of hippocampal network activity throughout development and evolution
node_relevance_reason: Uses multi-institute integrated scRNA-seq dataset to confirm Sst-IN transcriptomic identity and discovers Grin3a expression in NGFCs; classifies IvCs as Lamp5+/Lhx6+ MGE-derived and CGE-derived NGFCs as Lamp5+/Lhx6-; highly relevant to P2 (transcriptomic bridging) and P3 (IvC vs NGC boundary)
summary: Multi-institute integrated scRNA-seq of 86,852 GABAergic interneuron transcriptomes confirmed that most Sst-INs express high Grin3a and discovered Grin3a expression across most NGFC subsets. Ivy cells (IvCs) are classified as Lamp5+/Lhx6+ (MGE-derived), while CGE-derived NGFCs are Lamp5+/Lhx6-/Ndnf+. In vivo, mClover-GluN3A labelled cells co-expressed Sst in stratum oriens and nNOS in putative NGFCs in stratum lacunosum moleculare.
quotes: Amongst NGFCs Grin3a is expressed in both Lamp5 + /Lhx6 + MGE-derived subsets, also known as Ivy cells (IvyCs), and Lamp5 + /Lhx6 -caudal ganglionic eminence (CGE)-derived subsets that are both well represented in the rodent hippocampus

mClover-GluN3A labeled cells co-expressed Sst in stratum oriens and neuronal nitric oxide synthase (nNOS) in putaZve NGFCs that comprise the dominant interneuron subtype in stratum lacunosum moleculare

source_title: Discrete interneuron subsets participate in GluN1/GluN3A excitatory glycine receptor (eGlyR)-mediated regulation of hippocampal network activity throughout development and evolution
section: Multi-institute single-cell transcriptome analyses of GABAergic interneurons
node_relevance_reason: Provides canonical marker gene combinations used for interneuron subtype annotation in the multi-institute scRNA-seq dataset; MGE markers (Lhx6, Pvalb, Sst, Id2) and CGE markers (Sncg, Vip, Calb2, Meis2, Cnr1, Ntng1, Ndnf); NGFC.M = Lhx6+/Lamp5+/Id2+; NGFC.C = Lhx6-/Lamp5+/Id2+/Ndnf+
summary: The multi-institute scRNA-seq dataset (86,852 GABAergic transcriptomes) used established marker gene combinations: MGE-derived interneurons were annotated by Gad1+ combined with Lhx6, Pvalb, Sst, Id2; CGE-derived by Lhx6-, Sncg, Vip, Calb2, Meis2, Cnr1, Ntng1, Ndnf. MGE-derived NGFCs were annotated Lhx6+/Lamp5+/Id2+ (NGFC.M); CGE-derived NGFCs as Lhx6-/Lamp5+/Id2+/Ndnf+ (NGFC.C).
quotes: The Neurogliaform interneurons that are Lhx6 + /Lamp5 + /Id2 + are annotated as MGE-derived (NGFC.M); and those that are Lhx6 - /Lamp5 + /Id2 + /Ndnf + are annotated as CGE-derived (NGFC.C)

source_title: Behavior‐dependent activity patterns of GABAergic long‐range projecting neurons in the rat hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Provides key context for hippocampo-septal (HS) cell characterisation: distinguishes SOM-expressing long-range projecting cells from locally terminating SOM interneurons (OLM, bistratified); directly relevant to P5 (HS cell) and P1 (soma location in stratum oriens)
summary: In rat CA1, at least five distinct SOM-expressing GABAergic interneuron types have been defined, innervating different proximo-distal dendritic domains of pyramidal cells. In addition to locally terminating SOM interneurons (OLM, bistratified), a diverse population of long-range SOM-expressing GABAergic neurons projects to the medial septum and/or retrohippocampal cortical areas.
quotes: In the rat hippocampal CA1 area, at least five distinct types of SOMexpressing GABAergic neuron have been defined
a diverse population of longrange SOM-expressing GABAergic neurons send projections to the medial septum and/or retrohippocampal cortical areas

source_title: Dendritic Inhibition Provided by Interneuron-Specific Cells Controls the Firing Rate and Timing of the Hippocampal Feedback Inhibitory Circuitry
node_relevance_reason: General background on hippocampal interneuron diversity and cell-type-specific activity; provides context but no specific data on individual interneuron types in the NODE CONTEXT
summary: Background on hippocampal interneuron diversity: different subtypes provide perisomatic vs dendritic inhibition to pyramidal cells, and interneurons show cell-type-specific and activity-dependent recruitment during hippocampal oscillations in different behavioural states.
quotes: Some interneuron subtypes provide perisomatic inhibition to pyramidal cells (PCs), whereas others innervate different dendritic domains of PCs.

source_title: Common Origins of Hippocampal Ivy and Nitric Oxide Synthase Expressing Neurogliaform Cells
node_relevance_reason: Provides lineage context: nNOS interneurons do not overlap with PV or SOM; NNos strongly colocalises with NPY in IvCs and a subset of NGCs; supports nNOS marker discussions for NGC/IvC nodes
summary: In the hippocampus, nNOS-expressing interneurons do not overlap with PV or SOM interneurons and largely segregate from CR interneurons. nNOS strongly co-localises with NPY in both IvCs and a subset of NGCs; based on laminar positioning, IvCs and NGCs are considered distinct subtypes but share electrophysiological, anatomical and molecular properties suggesting they may belong to the same family.
quotes: in the hippocampus, the neuronal isoform of nitric oxide synthase (nNOS) is expressed in an interneuron subpopulation that does not overlap with PV or SOM interneurons and also largely segregates from CR interneurons
nNOS strongly colocalizes with neuropeptide Y in both Ivy cells (IvCs) and a subset of neurogliaform cells (NGCs)

source_title: Discrete interneuron subsets participate in GluN1/GluN3A excitatory glycine receptor (eGlyR)-mediated regulation of hippocampal network activity throughout development and evolution
node_relevance_reason: Introduces Sst-IN eGlyR expression context and references multi-institute dataset; relevant to Sst-expressing interneurons broadly (OLM, bistratified, HS) but no specific subtype detail
summary: MGE-derived hippocampal interneurons express high Grin3a throughout development; adult Sst-INs exhibit robust eGlyR-mediated currents. The study uses transcriptional profiling, conditional loss-of-function, IHC, ex vivo electrophysiology and optogenetics to show Sst-INs and NGFCs express functional eGlyRs from early postnatal through adult.
quotes: adult somatostaZn-expressing interneurons (Sst-INs) exhibit robust eGlyRmediated currents

source_title: Behavior‐dependent activity patterns of GABAergic long‐range projecting neurons in the rat hippocampus
node_relevance_reason: Notes multiple SOM-expressing interneuron types in stratum oriens (OLM, bistratified, basket, axo-axonic, long-range projecting); provides in vivo recording context for SOM+ cells, relevant to multiple nodes
summary: Unlabelled interneurons recorded in stratum oriens may include OLM cells, horizontal bistratified, basket and axo-axonic cells, and various long-range projecting GABAergic cell types. Trilaminar cells (distinct projection neurons) were excluded by their unique high-frequency burst firing. The study compared temporal firing patterns of SOM+ projection cells with locally terminating bistratified and OLM cells.
quotes: The group of unlabeled interneurons recorded in stratum oriens may include O-LM cells, horizontal bistratified, basket and axo-axonic cells and various long-range projecting GABAergic cell types
